cohorts.name: MERCHANT & CONTENDER SILICON
cohorts.thesis: The non-anointed semis, each carrying its own story: AMD the contender bid for AI share, ARM the royalty layer under everyone's CPUs, INTC a turnaround wearing a foundry question, TXN analog chips into factories and cars — the closest thing silicon has to a real-economy tell.
cohorts.watch: INTC Jul 23 · TXN guided Jul 22 — its guide IS the industrial-demand read

cohorts.name: SEMI EQUIPMENT & MEMORY
cohorts.thesis: One step behind the accelerators: wafer-fab equipment (AMAT/ASML/KLAC/LRCX) gets bought when fabs expand, and the memory supercycle (MU HBM stacked on every GPU, SNDK NAND) is priced off AI demand for bytes. The cohort runs HOTTER than the front-line silicon in both directions — this is where the board's parabolas live, so the extension gates matter most here.
cohorts.watch: KLAC Jul 28 · LRCX Jul 29 · MU/SNDK vs the +40% ring before any entry

cohorts.name: POWER & GRID
cohorts.thesis: The physical bottleneck: AI datacenters need firm power, turbines and cooling before a single token is served, so hyperscaler capex reads through to these order books one step behind the silicon — and unlike the silicon, supply here is gated by grid interconnects and multi-year build times, not fab capacity.
cohorts.watch: VRT reports inside the week at very high IV — extension check before any entry

cohorts.name: AI SILICON
cohorts.thesis: The arms dealers: the hyperscalers' ~$725B of 2026 capex (+77% YoY, >$1T modeled for 2027) IS this cohort's order book — NVDA GPUs, AVGO and MRVL custom ASICs, TSM wafers, ANET the switching fabric. It trades as ONE block: TSMC beat every metric this month and the complex sold off anyway, so a capex-sentiment turn hits every name at once.
cohorts.watch: hyperscaler capex guides Jul 29–30 read straight through · NVDA Aug 26 · MRVL Aug 27 · AVGO Sep 3

cohorts.name: OLD-ECONOMY BELLWETHERS
cohorts.thesis: The real economy with no AI multiple attached: CAT machinery and infrastructure (quietly also a datacenter winner via power generation), WMT the consumer's checkout line, LIN industrial gases (production volumes in a bottle), PLD logistics real estate (the flow of physical goods). When THESE lead, the rotation is real and not a tech story.
cohorts.watch: WMT consumer color · CAT infrastructure orders

cohorts.name: THE SECTOR TAPE
cohorts.thesis: The rotation long tail — one chip per sector so a leadership turn is visible at a glance; the HEATMAP deck owns the detail. XLU doubles as the AI-power trade's regulated cousin; XHB is the purest rate-sensitivity tell on the board.
cohorts.watch: XLU vs XLK for the defensive turn · XHB when the rate question moves

cohorts.name: PLATFORMS — THE HYPERSCALERS
cohorts.thesis: The capex referendum. GOOG set the pattern Jul 22: revenue beat, Cloud +82%, and the stock FELL on doubled capex, negative free cash flow and a $40B ATM. GAAP EPS is distorted by private-AI markups ($99B OI&E at Alphabet) — the tape grades capex + FCF, not the EPS headline. AAPL is the cohort's odd one out: its bear case is the ABSENCE of an AI story, not the cost of one.
cohorts.watch: META + MSFT Jul 29 · AAPL + AMZN Jul 30 — four guides inside 30 hours

cohorts.name: BREADTH — THE OTHER 490
cohorts.thesis: The escape hatch. The top ten names are roughly a third of SPY, so a mega-cap de-rate drags the index mechanically UNLESS money rotates down-cap instead of leaving. RSP versus SPY is the single ratio that says which one is happening: broadening (bullish for everything else) or contraction.
cohorts.watch: RSP/SPY through the Jul 29–30 cluster · IWM vs its 200-day

cohorts.name: FINANCIALS & CREDIT
cohorts.thesis: The market's honest systemic gauge lives here: high-yield spreads price stress before equities do, and OAS tight-and-not-widening is the bond market voting that the capex fear is a cohort story, not a solvency story. Higher-for-longer helps bank margins; the tell to respect is OAS turning while the mega-caps print.
cohorts.watch: the OAS line on RISK through the Jul 29–30 cluster · KRE for regional stress

cohorts.name: HEALTHCARE
cohorts.thesis: The defensive shelf plus the one supercycle with no AI capex attached: GLP-1 (LLY carries it). Earnings without a datacenter bill — the classic rotation target if the AI complex de-rates and money still wants growth.
cohorts.watch: XBI as the biotech risk-appetite tell

cohorts.name: RATES — THE REGIME-SETTER
cohorts.thesis: Every long-duration asset — bonds AND growth multiples — prices off this room. A hawkish Warsh Fed with inflation ~4.2% and hike dots live keeps duration heavy and makes the Fed put weaker than the 2016–21 reflexes assume: dips get rescued slower, so V-recovery backtests from that era carry a caveat.
cohorts.watch: FOMC Jul 29, 2pm ET — the presser at 2:30 is usually the move

cohorts.name: SOFTWARE · CLOUD
cohorts.thesis: The cohort the AI question cuts AGAINST: does agentic AI expand seat counts or replace them, while the compute bill eats the budget that used to buy app-layer software? Until that resolves, every seat-based SaaS multiple carries a discount the infrastructure names don't. The security pair (CRWD/PANW) is the defensive corner — the last budget line a CIO cuts.
cohorts.watch: MSFT Jul 29 (Copilot attach) · NOW printed Jul 22 — its guide is the cohort's tell

cohorts.name: HARD ASSETS
cohorts.thesis: The non-correlated shelf: gold is the safe-haven bid (its fear-greed component read 92/100 while the tape sat calm — someone is hedging), and oil cash flows are the one earnings stream with no AI capex question attached to them.
cohorts.watch: GLD vs its 200-day if the cluster goes badly — the quiet-hedge confirmation

cohorts.name: MEGA-CAP CONSUMER
cohorts.thesis: The mega-caps whose bear case is NOT the capex referendum: NFLX trades on margin now that it no longer reports subscribers, TSLA on auto gross margin ex-credits and the robotaxi narrative. Same index weight, same cluster weeks — a different question entirely.
cohorts.watch: TSLA printed Jul 22 — margin ex-credits is the line that moves it

cohorts.name: CRYPTO & RETAIL RISK
cohorts.thesis: The speculative thermometer. Bitcoin flow and crypto-broker volume lead retail risk appetite, and this shelf sitting far under its 200-days while the indexes hold near highs is fear-side information hiding inside a calm tape. SOFI is the bank-charter fintech twin — 0.75-correlated with HOOD in the book's own one-bet detector, reporting the same day.
cohorts.watch: HOOD + SOFI print Jul 29 · IBIT vs its 200-day

cohorts.name: THE LEVERAGED AI BUILD
cohorts.thesis: The build-out financed with debt instead of free cash flow: ORCL buying OCI backlog with borrowing, CRWV the pure-play GPU landlord. This is where the AI-capex thesis meets the credit market first — the board's turn-marker and canary pair: if AI credit cracks, it shows here before the mega-caps feel it.
cohorts.watch: CRWV vs its 200-day · HY OAS on RISK · ORCL Sep 9
